DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the second cause of action for dangerous condition of public property in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Michael Ghadieh is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. As decided previously by the Court, plaintiff is barred from bringing the dangerous condition cause of action because it is based on a completely different factual theory than that alleged in Mr. Ghadieh's government claim. Mr. Ghadieh was required to file an application to amend his initial government claim pursuant to Gov't Code 911.4 after he allegedly learned the accurate location of the accident on or about July 6, 2017 but failed to do so. Even if the Court assumes that plaintiff was not required to file a leave to amend claim within one year and that the Gov't Code 650.4 exception may apply, Mr. Ghadieh fails to allege facts to show that that he exercised reasonable diligence in investigating the claim within the initial six-month time period.

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email in compliance with the court's tentative ruling. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Continued To Feb-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Plaintiff Cause Of Action For Dangerous Condition Of Public Property
Off Calendar Nov-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Hunter Sims III, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3800 of Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Demurrer To Plaintiff Cause Of Action For Dangerous Condition Of Public Property.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the second cause of action for dangerous condition of public property in the complaint filed by plaintiff Michael Ghadieh is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The dangerous condition of public property cause of action is based on a completely different factual theory than that alleged in Mr. Ghadieh's government claim. The facts in the claim delineating the bus driver's negligence do not put the City on notice regarding plaintiff's allegations for the City's negligence in constructing and maintaining the public road. This instant case is more like Fall River Joint Unified School Dist. V. Superior Court 206 Cal.App.3d 431 than Smith v. County of Los Angeles (1989) 214 Cal.App.3d 266. Nonetheless, Gov't Code 950.4 allows Mr. Ghadieh to bring the second cause of action. Accordingly, the demurrer is sustained with twenty days leave to amend for Mr. Ghadeih to allege facts that despite his reasonable diligence in investigating the claim, he neither knew nor had a reason to know, within the period for the claim presentation, that the dangerous condition was a substantial fact in causing the accident. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: John K. Stewart, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302)
